title: Abort from `GenericAlias.__sizeof__`: `ob->ob_type != &PyLong_Type`
author: devdanzin
body:
# Crash report

### What happened?

It's possible to abort the interpreter in 3.12 by calling `__sizeof__` of a `GenericAlias` instance:

```python
from types import GenericAlias

g = GenericAlias(BaseException, Exception)
g.__sizeof__(1)
```
Abort message:
```
python: ./Include/object.h:231: Py_SIZE: Assertion `ob->ob_type != &PyLong_Type' failed.
Aborted
```
Found using [fusil](https://github.com/devdanzin/fusil) by @vstinner.

### CPython versions tested on:

3.12

### Operating systems tested on:

Linux

### Output from running 'python -VV' on the command line:

Python 3.12.8+ (heads/3.12:8e3c2d2, Dec  4 2024, 10:58:33) [GCC 11.4.0]

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body: I created https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/127605 backport to fix the issue.

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body: Fixed by commit https://github.com/python/cpython/commit/0964f9f1847bcbe90345eeafd65813d06db4ba3b. Thanks for your bug report.
